servinfo.title: Stall Test
servinfo.ptxt: This test is performed to check overall performance of CVT and engine by measuring stall speed in D and R ranges.

attention4.ptxt: If proper precautions are not taken when performing stall tests, a CVT failure may result due to an excessive rise in CVT fluid temperature.

item.ptxt: Do not continue running the engine for longer than 5 seconds at stall speed.
After performing a stall test, be sure to allow the engine to run at idle for longer than 1 minute before performing another stall test.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC.
Select “Data list” mode on scan tool.
Drive vehicle so that CVT fluid temperature reaches normal operating temperature (60 to 80 °C (140 to 176 °F)).

attention3.ptxt: Engine coolant temperature rises when revving engine in P or N range, but CVT fluid temperature does not rise in P or N range. Be sure to drive vehicle in order to raise CVT fluid temperature.

s1.ptxt: Apply parking brake and block wheels.
Start engine with select lever shifted to P range.
Shift select lever to D range and depress accelerator pedal fully. Read engine rpm quickly when it has become constant (stall speed).
Release accelerator pedal immediately after stall speed is checked.
In the same way, check stall speed in R range.
Stall speed should be within the following specification.

condition.ptxt: Lower than standard level in both D and R ranges
Higher than standard level only in D range
Higher than standard level only in R range
Higher than standard level in both D and R ranges
